briefdescription.para: To indicate queue full state.
To indicate Client is ready.
ASU IPI interrupt mask.
ASUFW check timeout value.
KAT execution status check timeout value in microseconds (5sec)
No of contexts can be saved by client.
Initial count for multi-update requests.
Invalid unique id value.
Entry of callback info.
ASU Context with initial UniqueId value as XASU_INVALID_UNIQUE_ID_VAL for all contexts
This function returns an instance pointer of ASU client interface.

listitem.para: Pointer to the client instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sends an IPI request to ASU.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if the IPI is sent successfully.
XST_FAILURE, if there is a failure.

briefdescription.para: This function polls for the response from ASUFW.

parameterdescription.para: Callback reference pointer.

inbodydescription.para: Search for the buffer index whose response is ready.
Get UniqueID.
Copy the response buffer data if any.
Perform callback to application to indicate the operation is complete.
Copy additional status from response buffer.
Clear the callback info upon completion.
Clear request and response status.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the ASUFW application's present status.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if ASUFW is present.
XST_FAILURE, if ASUFW is not present.

briefdescription.para: This function generates a unique ID for client requests.

detaileddescription.para: The client library assigns a unique ID to each request, ensuring distinct identification. This ID is used to map the application's response handlers, which are invoked when the corresponding response IPI interrupt is received from ASUFW.

simplesect.para:
Unique ID
0 to (XASU_UNIQUE_ID_MAX-1) if AsuCallBackRef array has any empty index.
XASU_UNIQUE_ID_MAX upon no free space is left

briefdescription.para: This function gets the free index of selected priority queue buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Select the queue priority.

simplesect.para:
Free Index of the Channel queue buffer of the selected priority
0 to 7
XASU_MAX_BUFFERS, if no index is free

inbodydescription.para: Get the next free index.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the client instance.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the Mailbox instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, On successful initialization.
XST_FAILURE, On failure.

inbodydescription.para: Validate client instance pointer.
Check for ASUFW present bit.
Initialize performance measurement.
Map the IPI shared memory of the channel.

briefdescription.para: This function validates the input client parameters.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XAsu_ClientParams instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if client parameters validation is successful.
XASU_INVALID_ARGUMENT, if the input parameters are invalid.
XASU_INVALID_CLIENT_PARAM, if ClientParamPtr is NULL.
XASU_INVALID_CALL_BACK_REF, if CallBackFuncPtr is NULL.
XASU_INVALID_PRIORITY, if invalid priority is selected.

inbodydescription.para: Validate that the callback function pointer is not NULL.
Validate the priority.
Validate the secure flag.

briefdescription.para: This function updates the queue buffer status to notify the request is present and generates a doorbell to ASU.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XAsu_ClientParams instance.
Pointer to the XAsu_ChannelQueueBuf's Request buffer data to be filled.
Size of the request buffer in bytes.
Header of the request buffer to be filled.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if IPI is sent successfully.
XST_FAILURE, if there is any failure.
XASU_INVALID_ARGUMENT, if the input parameters are invalid.

inbodydescription.para: Validate input parameters.
Get free queue buffer index of selected priority
Copy the request buffer to the free index.
Set IsCmdPresent to XASU_TRUE to indicate the command is present in the queue.
Place an IPI request to ASU.
If sending IPI fails, clean up the queue buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function registers callback parameters across the generated unique ID per request.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XAsu_ClientParams structure which holds the client input arguments.
Buffer to hold the response from response buffer, if no data is part of response buffer for the request the value shall be NULL.
Size of the data to be filled in response buffer.
Flag indicating whether this is the final callback (1 if final, 0 otherwise).

listitem.para: Unique ID used for registration (valid values are 0 to (XASU_UNIQUE_ID_MAX - 1)).
XASU_UNIQUE_ID_MAX, if unique ID is unavailable.

inbodydescription.para: Generate unique ID for the request.
Store the callback details.

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 UniqueId, u8 *RespBufferPtr, u32 Size, u8 IsFinalCall)
memberdef.name: XAsu_UpdateCallBackDetails

briefdescription.para: Updates callback details based on the provided unique ID, response buffer, size, and final call flag.

parameterdescription.para: Unique identifier for the callback update.
Pointer to the response buffer containing the data.
Size of the response buffer.
Flag indicating whether this is the final callback (1 if final, 0 otherwise).

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if callback details are updated successfully.
XST_FAILURE, in case of failure.

inbodydescription.para: Validate unique ID.
Update the callback details.

briefdescription.para: This function fetches the algorithm information based on Module ID and populates the algorithm information structure.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure where the algorithm information of module will be stored.
Registered ID of the module.

listitem.para:
XST_SUCCESS, if version info is updated successfully.
XST_FAILURE, if version info update fails.
XASU_KAT_EXEC_NOT_COMPLETED, if KAT execution is not completed.
XASU_INVALID_ARGUMENT, if any argument is invalid.

inbodydescription.para: Validate input parameters.
Wait until KAT execution is completed.

briefdescription.para: Frees the memory associated with the provided context.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XAsu_ClientCtx to be freed.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if the context is freed successfully.
XASU_FREE_CTX_FAIL, if freeing the context fails.

briefdescription.para: Saves the provided unique ID and returns the address of the saved context.

parameterdescription.para: Unique ID associated with the request

simplesect.para: Pointer of the XAsu_ClientCtx, which references the saved context.

inbodydescription.para: Validate unique id.
Store the context based on unique id.

briefdescription.para: Validates the provided context by comparing it with stored contexts to determine the appropriate multi-update calls.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XAsu_ClientCtx reference context.
Pointer to the buffer where the corresponding Unique ID will be stored.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if validation is successful.
XASU_INVALID_CLIENT_CTX if the provided context is not found.

briefdescription.para: Handles INIT or UPDATE/FINAL operations for multi-part cryptographic operations with priority-based context management.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XAsu_ClientParams structure with priority information.
Pointer to HIGH priority (P0) context.
Pointer to LOW priority (P1) context.
Current operation flags (combination of XASU_INIT, XASU_UPDATE, XASU_FINISH) from the client request.
Pointer to store the generated or retrieved Unique ID.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if operation is successful.
XASU_INVALID_PRIORITY, if the priority in ClientParamPtr is invalid.
XASU_INVALID_UNIQUE_ID, if generated Unique ID is invalid.
XASU_REQUEST_INPROGRESS, if a multi-part request is already in progress.
XASU_FAIL_SAVE_CTX, if context save fails.
XASU_CLIENT_CTX_NOT_CREATED, if client context does not exist.

inbodydescription.para: Check if this is an INIT operation.
Determine context pointer based on priority.
Check if context already exists for this priority level.
Allow only single-shot operation when context already exists.
Create new context for new multi-part or single-shot operation.
Save context and update client context pointer.
Handle UPDATE/FINAL operation - verify that context exists.

memberdef.argsstring: (XAsu_ClientParams *ClientParamPtr, void **P0CtxPtr, void **P1CtxPtr, u8 UniqueId, u8 *ResponseBuffer, u32 ResponseSize)
memberdef.name: XAsu_CleanupFinishOperation

briefdescription.para: Cleans up context and updates callback details when a FINISH/FINAL operation is executed.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XAsu_ClientParams structure.
HIGH priority (P0) context.
LOW priority (P1) context.
Unique ID associated with this operation.
Pointer to response buffer address (can be NULL).
Size of the response buffer.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if cleanup is successful.
Error code from callback update or context free.

briefdescription.para: This file contains the ASU client initialization and generic queue management functions.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 vns 06/03/24 Initial release ma 07/17/24 Update P0, P1 Queue addresses and set IsCmdPresent to TRUE before triggering the IPI interrupt to ASU ss 08/13/24 Changed XAsu_ClientInit function prototype and Initialized mailbox in XAsu_ClientInit() API ss 09/19/24 Added XAsu_CheckAsufwPrsntBit() API yog 09/26/24 Added doxygen groupings and fixed doxygen comments. vns 09/30/24 Added support for asynchronous communication 1.1 ma 01/08/25 Clear only ReqBufStatus and RespBufStatus upon command completion vns 02/06/25 Fixed magic numbers ma 02/19/25 Updated handling of same priority queue requests in round robin scheduling am 03/05/25 Added performance measurement init call ma 03/14/25 Replace memcpy with Xil_SecureMemCpy to avoid arch dependencies during copy kd 07/23/25 Fixed gcc warnings am 08/08/25 Removed redundant condition before END label rmv 09/15/25 Updated XAsu_UpdateCallBackDetails() prototype to return status value rmv 09/20/25 Updated memory copy logic to store Xil_SecureMemCpy return value in local variable to improve readability. rmv 09/21/25 Used unique id as an index for AsuContext
